REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO STATE ANY VIABLE CLAIMS AGAINST THE DEFENDANTS IN CLEAR AND CONCISE LANGUAGE; COMPLAINT MUST INCLUDE A DEMAND FOR JUDGMENT; EACH CAUSE OF ACTION MUST BE CLEARLY LABELED AND INDICATE WHICH DEFENDANT IT IS ALLEGED AGAINST; EXHIBITS MUST BE CLEARLY LABELED AND REFERENCED IN THE COMPLAINT BY ITS DESIGNATED LABEL (E.G. EX A-Z OR EX. 1-20). PARTIES MUST COMPLY WITH ALL PROCEDURAL RULES REGARDING DRAFTING THE PLEADINGS (E.G. CCP 425.10; CRC 2.112) AND REGARDING THE FORMAT OF BRIEFS (CRC 3.1113). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B) JUDGE: CHARLES F, HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. INSUFFICIENT NOTICE PER CCP 1005 AND 1010.6(A)(3)(B). ADDITIONALLY, THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT ACCOMPANIED BY A DECLARATION RE: MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS AS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IN-PERSON OR OVER THE PHONE AT A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE TIME NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 13, 2024. IF PARTIES CAN NOT AGREE ON A DATE A TIME, PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER ON DECEMBER 5, 2024 AT 1:30 P.M. NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 16, 2024 PARTIES TO FILE A DECLARATION ATTESTING TO THEIR GOOD FAITH PARTICIPATION IN MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS. DEFENDANT MAY FILE A DEMURRER IF NECESSARY AFTER THE MEET AND CONFER NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 17, 2024. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHY ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Relief From Default And Extension Of Time To File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALISON THOMAS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT AND EXTENSION OF TIME TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff's request for relief is premature because there is no default or dismissal entered. Additionally, Plaintiff's requested deadline to amend the Complaint is April 5 - a date that has already passed.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Defendant's Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to state any viable claims against the Defendants in clear and concise language; complaint must include a demand for judgment; each cause of action must be clearly labeled and indicate which defendant it is alleged against; exhibits must be clearly labeled and referenced in the complaint by its designated label (e.g. Ex A-Z or Ex. 1-20). Parties must comply with all procedural rules regarding drafting the pleadings (e.g. CCP 425.10; CRC 2.112) and regarding the format of briefs (CRC 3.1113).

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F, Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Alison Thomas, plaintiff in pro per, via zoom
Sheila Ho Esq., Phillips, Spallas & Angstadt LLP, 415-278-9400 via zoom for defendant

Ms. Ho contests the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ERNEST ANTONIS, MAKRAS REAL ESTATE, VICTOR MAKRAS DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1010.6(a)(3)(B). Additionally, the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is not accompanied by a declaration re: meet and confer efforts as to the First Amended Complaint. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in-person or over the phone at a mutually agreeable time no later than December 13, 2024. If parties can not agree on a date a time, parties shall meet and confer on December 5, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. No later than December 16, 2024 parties to file a declaration attesting to their good faith participation in meet and confer efforts. Defendant may file a demurrer if necessary after the meet and confer no later than December 17, 2024.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachy ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
